channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Donald Trump Criticizes England's Tactical Approach in World Cup Defeat
item.description: US President Donald Trump joined the chorus of critics targeting England's tactical decisions during their FIFA World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina. Speaking at a reception in New York alongside FI...

item.title: DNA Test Reveals 38-Year-Old Birth Switch Lawsuit Against North Dakota Hospital
item.description: Two men, Kyle Bylin and Jeremy Morrison, discovered through DNA tests that they were switched at birth 38 years ago, leading their families to sue Unity Medical Center in North Dakota. The lawsuit all...

item.title: Dorset Council Faces Conflict Over Winfrith Nuclear Site Restoration Plans
item.description: The article discusses the proposed restoration of the former Winfrith nuclear site to natural heathland, a plan by the Nuclear Decommissioning Agency that has sparked controversy with neighboring Inno...

item.title: Java House Foundation Launches 35 Fully Funded Scholarships for Young Kenyans in Coffee Sector
item.description: Java House Foundation, in partnership with Dedan Kimathi University of Technology (DeKUT), has launched a scholarship program offering 35 fully funded opportunities for Kenyan youths aged 18-22. The i...

item.title: Grenade Attack During BARMM Election Gun Ban Injures Five
item.description: A grenade attack injured five people on the third day of the election gun ban for the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM) parliamentary elections, which are scheduled for September...

item.title: Community warned about child protection and infrastructure development
item.description: The article highlights two key issues affecting Tanzanian society. First, authorities are emphasizing the need for parents to safeguard children from abuse, stressing that guardians must remain vigila...

item.title: CTD Conducts Successful Intelligence Operation in Dera Ismail Khan, Kills Wanted Terrorist
item.description: The Counter Terrorism Department (CTD) conducted a targeted intelligence-based operation in Dera Ismail Khan district, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, resulting in the death of a wanted terrorist identified as Kh...

item.title: Multiple Injuries Reported After Stage 12 Tour de France Crash
item.description: Following a high-speed crash during the final kilometer of stage 12 of the Tour de France, multiple riders sustained injuries, with several forced to withdraw from the race. The incident occurred as r...

item.title: Neil Rimer Advocates for Wealth Redistribution in Tech Amid Rising AI Billionaire Wealth
item.description: The article explores Neil Rimer's perspective on wealth concentration within the tech industry, particularly among AI billionaires. As a prominent investor at Index Ventures, Rimer highlights concerns...

item.title: William Troost-Ekong Plans to Influence Football Governance from Boardroom
item.description: Former Nigerian captain William Troost-Ekong, who ended his international career in 2025 after 83 caps, has revealed his intention to transition into football administration post-retirement. Ekong, wh...

item.title: A Novella's Bold Exploration of Resistance Against Caste and Patriarchy
item.description: Abha Purbey's novella 'Gulabiya', translated from the Angika language into English, explores themes of personal rebellion against caste and patriarchy. The story follows Gulabiya, a young farm laborer...

item.title: A centenarian's secret to longevity: positivity and roast beef
item.description: The article highlights the lifestyle of a centenarian from Timmins, Ontario, who has lived to 100 years by embracing positivity and enjoying roast beef. Lydia Chubak, the author, notes that the indivi...

item.title: Affordable Starter Cities in the UK: A Guide for First-Time Buyers and Renters
item.description: The article highlights several UK cities—Southampton, Cardiff, Glasgow, and Norwich—as 'starter cities' offering affordable housing options for first-time buyers and renters. Southampton stands out fo...

item.title: Police officer demonstrates integrity by refusing bribe in drug operation
item.description: A sergeant from Mitchell's Plain Crime Prevention Unit has been praised for refusing a bribe and arresting a suspect during a drug operation. The incident occurred on July 15 when the officer received...

item.title: Conservation Efforts in Apayao for Philippine Eagle Protection
item.description: The article discusses the inauguration of the Apayao Biosphere Reserve Research Center, a collaborative effort between conservationists and the local government to protect the Philippine eagle. This c...

item.title: 63-Year-Old Bronx Man Arrested After Ramming Beyoncé and Jay-Z's East Hampton Home
item.description: A 63-year-old Bronx man was arrested after driving his vehicle through the security gates of Beyoncé and Jay-Z's East Hampton mansion, causing significant damage. Police reported that the man was spee...

item.title: Toronto Tempo's Turnover Plague Costs Them in Loss to Atlanta Dream
item.description: The Toronto Raptors' women's basketball team, the Toronto Tempo, suffered an 111-92 loss to the Atlanta Dream, exacerbated by 20 costly turnovers that the Dream converted into 29 points. Coach Sandy B...

item.title: Ancient Egyptian royal women's skeletal evidence suggests weapon use in daily life
item.description: A new study of royal skeletons from ancient Egypt's Twelfth Dynasty suggests that women, including princesses, may have regularly used weapons. The research, led by Zaineb Hashesh from the University ...

item.title: Delhi Police moves hunger striker Sonam Wangchuk to hospital amid protest tensions
item.description: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike since June 28, was forcibly removed from the Jantar Mantar protest site by Delhi Police on Saturday. The police claimed he was shifted to a hos...

item.title: Zanzibar Urges Contractors to Address Project Delays
item.description: The article highlights Zanzibar's recent warning to contractors regarding ongoing project delays. Officials have emphasized the need for timely completion of infrastructure projects to avoid further e...
